user_query: I'm a senior basketball player weighing scholarship offers. Please prepare contact-ready email drafts to men's basketball programs using the schools listed in input/target_programs.csv and my details in input/player_profile.json. I need you to: 1) Perform web searches to locate, for each school, its official men's basketball coaching staff page and the official athletics recruiting page (use only the school's official athletics site or the university's official site; avoid third-party directories/aggregators). Save the exact search queries and the chosen URLs per school in output/sources.json. 2) Download the two identified pages for each school and save the raw HTML to output/webpages/raw/<School>_{staff|recruiting}.html. Also extract the visible text of each page to output/webpages/text/<School>_{staff|recruiting}.txt so parsing is easier. Use a command-line downloader that emits HTTP status codes and error messages (e.g., curl or wget), and build output/fetch_log.json by analyzing the tool's output for each attempt with fields: school, page_type (staff|recruiting), url, http_status (int), ok (boolean), error_message (string; empty if none). 3) Parse the extracted text to find up to three relevant email addresses per school that appear to belong to the head coach, assistant coaches, or the program's recruiting contact. Write output/contacts.csv with columns: school, contact_name (if available), role (head coach|assistant|recruiting|unknown), email, source_page_type (staff|recruiting), source_file (relative path to the text file you parsed). 4) Create output/crosscheck_report.json verifying for each row in contacts.csv whether the email string appears in at least one of the saved files for that school (the corresponding raw HTML or extracted text); include fields: school, email, matched (boolean), matched_file (path or empty). 5) For each school, use input/player_profile.json to generate a personalized draft email saved to output/email_drafts/<School>.txt. Structure each draft as: - To: <primary_contact_email or UNKNOWN if none found> - Subject: <[School] Men's Basketball — Jordan Miles (2026 SF, 4.0 GPA)> - Body: a brief 5–8 sentence message that introduces me (name, position, height/weight, GPA), references that I reviewed their staff/recruiting info, includes my highlight link, cites a couple of key stats, and asks three specific questions (scholarship offer timeline, role/fit, and official visit availability using one of the visit_windows). Close with my contact info. If no email was found, set To: UNKNOWN and include one sentence saying I will submit or have submitted the official recruiting questionnaire if you found such a link. Requirements: - Use only official school/athletics sources for pages; if multiple candidates exist, choose the one that clearly represents the school's official athletics or university site. - The Internet steps (searching and downloading) are required; do not hardcode URLs. - Keep all outputs under the output/ directory as specified. - If a download fails or returns an HTTP status >= 400, still record the attempt in fetch_log.json with ok=false and continue with the other page for that school. - Ensure that the To address in each email draft matches the first (most relevant) email for that school in output/contacts.csv when at least one contact is found. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
